rationale: The HTML declares `id="theme-switch"` and `id="people-decrement"`. The JS calls `getElementById('theme-toggle')` and `getElementById('decrement-btn')`. Both files are individually valid: HTML parses cleanly, JS is syntactically fine, all interactive elements have id attributes, JS has plenty of `addEventListener` calls (so the `interactivity` layer also passes). The bug is the SEAM: HTML and JS were generated independently and settled on different naming conventions. At runtime, `getElementById` returns `null`, the listener is attached to a phantom node, and clicking does nothing. The `html_js_id_parity` layer cross-references every `#id` the JS hooks against the ids the HTML declares.

baseline_comparison.eslint: PARTIAL — eslint-plugin-html exists but cross-file id resolution is not its strength. No common preset enables it.
baseline_comparison.html_validator: PASS — both files are individually valid HTML/JS.
baseline_comparison.interactivity_count_check: PASS — JS has 4 event bindings, HTML has 5 interactive elements. Counting alone doesn't catch which ids they hook.
baseline_comparison.raw_llm_critique: UNRELIABLE — sometimes catches when files are small enough to keep in working memory, often misses.
baseline_comparison.manual_QA: Catches on first click — but the validator pipeline is supposed to obviate manual QA for routine bugs.
